company_type: Consumer Goods - Personal Care and Tissue Products

dependencies: Cellulose fiber (kraft pulp or recycled fiber) for tissue products
Fluff pulp for disposable diapers, training pants, feminine and incontinence products
Polypropylene and other synthetics for nonwoven fabrics
Superabsorbent materials for disposable diapers and incontinence products
Petroleum-based materials subject to price volatility
Sole-source and single-source suppliers for certain commodities and raw materials
risks: Significant price fluctuations in pulp and petroleum-based materials
Supplier constraints and supplier consolidation limiting sources of supply
Disruptions in supply chain due to natural disasters, power outages, fires, explosions, labor strikes, terrorism, epidemics, pandemics
Import restrictions and regional economic/political events
Sole-source supplier failures could adversely impact operations
Complex network of suppliers with potential disruptions
High input costs and supply chain complexities in Russia due to war in Ukraine
Restricted access to raw materials and production assets in Russia
Currency restrictions limiting ability to obtain currency exchange for U.S. dollar inputs

regulatory_risks: Product safety, efficacy, manufacturing, advertising, labeling and safety reporting regulations
Environmental protection statutes and regulations (water resources, wastewater discharge)
Expanding laws on sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence
Data privacy and protection laws (EU GDPR, Brazil GDPL, China PIPL, California CCPA)
Anti-corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act)
Product recalls if safety risks exist
Government trade or similar regulatory actions
Potential changes in U.S. trade programs and trade relations
Imposition of increased or new tariffs, sanctions, export controls, quotas, trade barriers
Climate change regulations and reporting requirements (including potential Anti-ESG legislation)
Tax law changes and new interpretations
OECD/G20 Pillar 2 Global anti-Base Erosion rules (15% minimum tax)
Compliance with various environmental protection laws across jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Russia sanctions impacting operations (supply chain, professional services, monetary, currency, trade and payment/investment sanctions)
Restricted access to financial institutions in Russia
Potential asset impairment in Russia if operations cannot be sustained
War in Ukraine causing cyber-based attacks, disruptions to foreign exchange rates and financial markets
Potential expropriation and political instabilities in foreign markets
Sanctions issued by U.S., European Union, Russia or other countries affecting operations
